name: Lone Wolf
description: A single commercial quadcopter (DJI-class) on direct approach toward the protected asset. Low altitude, single target. Work through the DTID kill chain: detect, track, identify, and defeat the threat.

name: Recon Probe
description: Three contacts arrive one at a time. The first is a bird — do NOT engage. The second is a fixed-wing on a reconnaissance pass across your airspace. The third is a commercial quad on direct approach. Test your identification discipline and ROE compliance.

name: Swarm Attack
description: Six contacts inbound from multiple directions. A mix of commercial quads, a fixed-wing, a micro drone, one Shahed-type autonomous drone (jam-immune), and one bird (false alarm). Staggered arrival — expect the second wave at T+30s and late contacts at T+60s. Prioritize threats, manage effectors, and don't waste resources on the bird.

name: Tutorial
description: A guided walkthrough of the DTID kill chain. A single slow-moving quadcopter approaches while step-by-step instructions guide you through detection, tracking, identification, and defeat.

name: Open Skies
description: All threats. No script. 20 minutes of escalating chaos — or go endless. Quadcopters, fixed-wing UAS, Shaheds, birds, and friendly jets. Three phases of increasing intensity test every skill in the DTID kill chain.
